title: Coarse Menger Conjecture (Conjecture 1.1)
statement_text: For all integers $k\geq 0$ and $c\geq 1$ there exists $\ell\geq 0$ with the following property. Let $G$ be a graph and let $S,T\subseteq V(G)$. Then either there are $k+1$ paths between $S,T$, pairwise at distance at least $c$; or there is a set $X\subseteq V(G)$ with $|X|\leq k$ such that every path between $S,T$ contains a vertex with distance at most $\ell$ from some member of $X$.
context_text: Two independent groups — Albrechtsen, Huynh, Jacobs, Knappe and Wollan, and Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu — proposed this coarse analogue of Menger's theorem. It was shown to be true for $k=1$ by both groups, and for bounded-degree graphs with $c=2$ by Gartland–Korhonen–Lokshtanov and Hendrey–Norin–Steiner–Turcotte; however, the same authors of the present paper disproved it for all $k\geq 2$ and $c\geq 3$ in an earlier work.
attributed_to: Albrechtsen, Huynh, Jacobs, Knappe, and Wollan; and independently Georgakopoulos and Papasoglu
paper_contribution: Proves the conjecture for all graphs of bounded path-width (and, further, for graphs of bounded coarse path-width); also notes the conjecture remains false for bounded tree-width.
paper_title: Asymptotic structure. V. The coarse Menger conjecture in bounded path-width

title: Informal conjecture: coarse Menger for bounded genus
statement_text: It might be true that the coarse Menger conjecture holds for graphs of bounded genus.
context_text: The counterexamples to the coarse Menger conjecture constructed in the authors' earlier paper have unbounded genus. The present paper proves the conjecture for bounded path-width; the authors note that the bounded-genus case is open and mention [13] for some progress in that direction.
notes: Stated as a possibility in running prose ("it might be true … but this is open"); no formal conjecture label.
paper_title: Asymptotic structure. V. The coarse Menger conjecture in bounded path-width
